prose: Fukui reactivity indices were computed for ferrocene (21 atoms: 10 C, 10 H, 1 Fe) using the GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding method (gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1) via chemkit/xtb. The geometry was taken from the user-supplied file ferrocene.xyz without re-optimization. Mulliken partial charges were used for the finite-difference Fukui scheme (N±1 states: cation charge +1 mult 2, anion charge −1 mult 2). The headline value reported is the most nucleophilic site's f⁻ = 0.25938191, located on Fe (atom 11). Fe is also the most electrophilic site (f⁺ = 0.22885456). The dual descriptor on Fe is −0.030527, confirming net nucleophilic character at the metal center. All Cp ring carbons and hydrogens are symmetry-equivalent within each ring and show small, nearly equal Fukui indices (~0.0136 for C and ~0.0635 for H). Integrity checks passed: sum(f⁺) = sum(f⁻) = 1.0000. No engine warnings were reported.
